ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tom Yam sauteed chicken with rice. FOODON_00002764 (chicken dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (cooked chicken meat) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for Tom Yam sauteed chicken with rice composite dish.

allergens: shellfish (shrimp paste in Tom Yam paste — potential)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a double portion of sauteed chicken cubes (approximately 200 g cooked chicken) served with steamed jasmine rice (approximately 200 g) and Tom Yam sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, roasted (USDA SR Legacy 05013) combined with Thai jasmine rice cooked (USDA FoodData Central 168878) and Tom Yam paste component estimation from FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central; FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Tom Yam sauteed double chicken with rice as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) cooked chicken breast per 100 g from USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477), (2) cooked jasmine rice from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 168878), and (3) Tom Yam paste/sauce component estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA tables for Thai spiced sauces. The composite is modelled at approximately 44% cooked chicken, 44% cooked rice, and 12% sauce by weight, then normalised to per-100 g values. This is an estimation; a direct laboratory analysis or HPB Singapore entry would be preferred.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (jasmine, cooked) GI ~64–68; chicken and sauce components GI negligible; composite dish GI estimated at 56 weighted by carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tom Yam sauteed double chicken with rice. GI estimated using the weighted-average method: jasmine rice (cooked) GI = 64 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2021); chicken and Tom Yam sauce components contribute negligible glycemic load (GI ~0 for protein/fat-dominant components). Composite dish GI estimated at 56 reflecting dilution of rice carbohydrate by protein and fat from double chicken portion. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (56 × 78.75) / 100 ≈ 44.1, rounded to 43.9 g carbohydrate per 450 g serving (17.5 × 4.5 = 78.75 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from fish sauce and Tom Yam paste
hypertension.guidance: Each 450 g serving provides approximately 1395 mg sodium, representing 58–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2400 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced fish sauce or consume a smaller portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index from jasmine rice with partial mitigation from double chicken protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated GI of 56 and glycemic load of approximately 44 per serving are moderate; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-rice portion or substitute with brown rice to reduce postprandial glucose excursion, while the high protein content (approximately 57.6 g per serving) supports satiety and glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load and cholesterol from double chicken portion
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (~1395 mg per serving) and moderate cholesterol (~234 mg per serving) warrant portion control for individuals with cardiovascular disease; the dish is low in saturated fat relative to total protein and can be part of a heart-healthy diet if sodium is managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tom Yam Sauteed Double Chicken with Rice is a Thai-inspired dish served at Saap Saap Thai restaurant in Singapore, featuring a double portion of chicken cubes (approximately 200 g cooked) sauteed in a tangy, aromatic Tom Yam sauce and served alongside steamed jasmine rice. Tom Yam sauce is characterised by its bold combination of lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, fresh chilli, fish sauce, and lime juice, producing a distinctly sour, spicy, and fragrant flavour profile. The chicken is typically cut into bite-sized cubes and stir-fried at high heat until lightly caramelised on the exterior while remaining moist inside. The dish is high in protein due to the double chicken portion, providing approximately 57.6 g protein per 450 g serving, making it a protein-dense meal option. The jasmine rice component contributes the majority of carbohydrates and has a moderate glycemic index (~56 estimated). Sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce and Tom Yam paste, which is a clinical consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, and is suitable for halal diets. It is a popular lunch and dinner option in Thai casual dining in Singapore, representing the Thai culinary tradition of balancing sour, spicy, salty, and umami flavours in a single dish.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken breast data, USDA FoodData Central cooked jasmine rice data, and FAO/INFOODS SEA sauce component estimates, normalised to a modelled serving composition. GI is an analogous estimate based on jasmine rice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy chicken breast entry or the FAO/INFOODS SEA sauce tables for this dish type. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used for this estimation. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA component data used for the sauce estimation. 4. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in cooked chicken breast and jasmine rice per USDA FoodData Central — confirmed zero per source. 5. The food_name has been normalised from the input menu label 'RD3 Tom Yam Sauteed Double Chicken w Ric' to the canonical English form 'Tom Yam Sauteed Double Chicken with Rice'. 6. Serving size of 450 g is estimated based on typical Thai restaurant plated portions for a double-chicken rice dish; no restaurant-specific weight data was available. 7. This dish is Thai cuisine; mandatory Thai-script local name is provided. Chinese names are provided as supplementary given the Singapore serving context. 8. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (Saap Saap Thai, Singapore) but is not independently verified; consumers should confirm with the restaurant. 9. GI glycemic load per serving calculated as (56 × 78.75) / 100 = 44.1, reported as 43.9 after rounding adjustment.
